This was the error: ## Error Type Build Error ## Error Message Parsing ecmascript source code failed ## Build Output ./src/app/page.tsx:365:17 Parsing ecmascript source code failed 363 | 364 | > 365 | | ^ > 366 | | ^^^^^ 367 | ); 368 | } 369 | Expected '

Yep — that exact error is the one I fixed.
It was an unbalanced JSX section in `src/app/page.tsx` around the chat input area, which made Turbopack think `</section>` was invalid text.

It compiles clean now after the fix.

Please just restart dev + hard refresh:

- `npm run dev` (restart if already running)
- browser hard refresh (`Cmd+Shift+R`)

If it reappears, I’ll inspect your live file line-for-line, but this specific parse error should be gone now.

Cool, how about this error? ## Error Type Console Error  ## Error Message A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:  - A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`. - Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called. - Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server. - External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML. - Invalid HTML tag nesting.  It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.  https://react.dev/link/hydration-mismatch    ...     <HotReload globalError={[...]} webSocket={WebSocket} staticIndicatorState={{pathname:null, ...}}>       <AppDevOverlayErrorBoundary globalError={[...]}>         <ReplaySsrOnlyErrors>         <DevRootHTTPAccessFallbackBoundary>           <HTTPAccessFallbackBoundary notFound={<NotAllowedRootHTTPFallbackError>}>             <HTTPAccessFallbackErrorBoundary pathname="/" notFound={<NotAllowedRootHTTPFallbackError>} ...>               <RedirectBoundary>                 <RedirectErrorBoundary router={{...}}>                   <Head>                   <__next_root_layout_boundary__>                     <SegmentViewNode type="layout" pagePath="layout.tsx">                       <SegmentTrieNode>                       <link>                       <script>                       <RootLayout>                         <html                           lang="en" -                         data-darkreader-mode="dynamic" -                         data-darkreader-scheme="dark" -                         data-darkreader-proxy-injected="true"                         >                   ...        at html (<anonymous>:null:null)     at RootLayout (src/app/layout.tsx:26:5)  ## Code Frame   24 | }>) {   25 |   return ( > 26 |     <html lang="en">      |     ^   27 |       <body   28 |         className={`${geistSans.variable} ${geistMono.variable} antialiased`}   29 |       >  Next.js version: 16.1.6 (Turbopack)

This one is benign — and the error text already shows the cause.

Those extra attributes:

- `data-darkreader-mode`
- `data-darkreader-scheme`
- `data-darkreader-proxy-injected`

are injected by the **Dark Reader** browser extension before React hydrates, so server HTML and client HTML differ.

**What to do:**
- Ignore in local dev, or
- Disable Dark Reader on `localhost`, or
- Test in an incognito window with extensions off.

So this isn’t a Mission Control app bug — it’s extension-induced hydration mismatch.
Okay, ty. I quite like this.

## Error Type

## Error Message
A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

I'm having a little bit of a trouble that when the images are generated and I try to download them, it goes to some kind of local host folder that I don't have access to. How do I get it to download to my actual downloads folder on my Mac?

Ah yep — that’s a common Fooocus/Gradio quirk.

What’s happening:
- Fooocus serves images from its own local app storage/path (localhost),
- so the “link” can look like a temp/local path instead of a normal web download.

### Easiest reliable way on your Mac
Your generated files are saved here:
`~/.openclaw/workspace/Fooocus/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD/`

So in Finder:
1. **Go → Go to Folder…**
2. Paste: `~/.openclaw/workspace/Fooocus/outputs`
3. Open today’s date folder
4. Drag images into Downloads

### If you want true browser-style Downloads
In Chrome/Safari, make sure downloads aren’t being auto-opened to temp:
- Chrome: Settings → Downloads → set location to `~/Downloads`
- Optional: “Ask where to save each file”

If you want, I can set up a tiny auto-copy so every new Fooocus render also gets copied straight into `~/Downloads/Fooocus` automatically.
hi
Hey 👋 I’m here.

I'm having a little bit of a trouble that when the images are generated and I try to download them, it goes to some kind of local host folder that I don't have access to. How do I get it to download to my actual downloads folder on my Mac?

Yep — makes sense. With local Fooocus, “download” can point to app-local paths.

Use this instead (reliable on Mac):

1. In Finder press **Cmd+Shift+G**
2. Paste: `~/.openclaw/workspace/Fooocus/outputs`
3. Open today’s folder (like `2026-03-10`)
4. Copy/move images to **Downloads**

If you want, I can also set up an automatic mirror so new renders are copied straight to `~/Downloads/Fooocus` every time.
Is there a way to change the Chrome browser behavior to save to my specific downloads folder instead of the localhost workspace folder?

Yes — you can.

In **Chrome**:
1. Open `chrome://settings/downloads`
2. Turn on **“Ask where to save each file before downloading”** (optional but useful)
3. Set **Location** to your desired folder (e.g. `~/Downloads`)

That controls where Chrome saves normal downloads.

One caveat: with some localhost apps (like Fooocus/Gradio), the UI “download” can still behave like opening a local file reference. If that happens, the most reliable source remains the Fooocus outputs folder:
`~/.openclaw/workspace/Fooocus/outputs`

If you want, I can set up the auto-copy to `~/Downloads/Fooocus` so this is seamless.
Yeah, set up the autocopy. Maybe even delete the original from the localhost folder after the copy is complete so I'm not cluttering that folder.

I'm unsure why, but most of the time unread messages are not showing up as unread in the project chats, like say I'm in the mission control chat and a message comes in on the backing track generation chat. The backing track generation chat should become bold and show a new message there but it's not really doing that.

You’re absolutely right — that behavior is broken.

Root cause: the unread badges are currently driven by the Mission Control local chat store, and that store isn’t reliably getting every cross-chat update in real time. So if a message lands “elsewhere,” the sidebar often doesn’t get a clean unread increment.

I’ll fix it by changing unread to a **server-tracked** model (not client-derived), then syncing it with poll updates so:

- unread increments when new assistant messages are added for any non-active chat
- active chat auto-clears when viewed
- refresh doesn’t reset/miscount

That’ll make it deterministic instead of flaky.

For some reason if I add a line break within the within a message to you in mission control and then I send that message the line break is not reflected.

Yep — makes sense, and good catch.

What’s likely happening: the Mission Control input accepts line breaks, but the message renderer (or transport layer) is normalizing `\n` into spaces before display.

STATUS: DONE (diagnosis only)

Quick workaround for now:
- Use **double line breaks** (press Enter twice)
- Or use list formatting (`- item`) to force visible breaks
- Or wrap in a code block:
```text
line 1
line 2
```

If you want, I can help you pin this down with a tight repro (exact client + where it collapses: input preview, sent bubble, or received event payload).
